commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a vendored Crabbox skill, `.crabbox.yaml`, a self-hosted Crabbox hydrate workflow, actionlint/CODEOWNERS files, and package scripts for Crabbox commands.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the copied skill references `scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs` and `.github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml`, while the repo file listing has neither. No runtime bug reproduction is otherwise applicable to this setup PR.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the branch reopens a setup-policy decision from the earlier broad PR and introduces a self-hosted runner boundary that automation cannot approve.

target.title: chore: add constrained Crabbox setup
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the exact Crabbox skill copied from `openclaw/openclaw`.
- Adds a constrained Crabbox config and hydrate workflow with repo-specific self-hosted runner labels.
- Adds actionlint label config, CODEOWNERS coverage for the new automation surfaces, and package scripts for the copied skill command surface.

This is the narrowed replacement shape for the earlier broad setup baseline. It intentionally does not add CodeQL, stale automation, licensing changes, Dependabot, or other policy-heavy defaults.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- Ruby YAML parse for `.crabbox.yaml`, `.github/actionlint.yaml`, and `.github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`
- `actionlint -config-file .github/actionlint.yaml .github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`
- Crabbox skill SHA-256 matched `openclaw/openclaw`: `ed512c0b0385fae7f6c5c14a7e9e6236ab68936506687a99ca976873492bdc43`
- Package script presence check for `check:changed`, `test:changed`, and `crabbox:*`
- Private-path scan for new public files
- `npm test` attempted; failed on 5 existing Node TS loader incompatibilities where child capture processes picked `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node/26.0.0/bin/node` and Node 26 rejects `stripTypeScriptTypes({ mode: "transform" })`.

## Notes

No live Crabbox lease was started for this setup-only patch.
target.author: vincentkoc
